nodes.label: Data Flow Diagram
nodes.summary: Top-level architecture diagram grouping models by product capability to show data flow across MongoDB, MySQL, and Redis stores.

nodes.label: Primary Store (MongoDB)
nodes.summary: MongoDB is the authoritative store for all live application data.

nodes.label: Derived Store (MySQL)
nodes.summary: MySQL derived store used for synchronized copies, comparisons, backups, and recovery workflows with data transformed from MongoDB.

nodes.label: Runtime Store (Redis)
nodes.summary: Redis runtime data includes cache entries, monitoring samples, rate-limit counters, alert throttles, and MongoDB-to-MySQL sync queue tasks.

nodes.label: Sync Service
nodes.summary: MongoDB-to-MySQL sync service that listens for MongoDB change events, serializes them into a Redis queue, and has a consumer write to MySQL.

nodes.label: Backup Service
nodes.summary: Scheduled backup service that runs daily: MongoDB at 02:00, MySQL at 02:30, and cleans expired backup files at 03:00.

nodes.label: Identity Data
nodes.summary: User and admin tokens and profiles used to authenticate protected flows.

nodes.label: Learning Content
nodes.summary: Period and Section documents plus reading task metadata exposed to public browsing, enrolled users, and admin content-management screens.

nodes.label: Enrollment and Payment
nodes.summary: Enrollment and Payment models covering access checks, payment initiation, confirmation, cancellation, WeChat callback handling, and membership status updates.

nodes.label: Participation Data
nodes.summary: Checkins, comments, and rankings that feed visibility, stats, and ranking features.

nodes.label: Insight Data
nodes.summary: Manual or generated insight documents, insight request workflows, approvals/rejections/revocation, likes, admin edits, and danmaku records.

nodes.label: Notification Data
nodes.summary: Notifications, subscribe grants, and deliveries that notify users and record delivery state.

nodes.label: Audit and Ops Data
nodes.summary: Admin write audit logs, user activity records, monitoring metrics, alert throttle keys, backup metadata, database comparison output, and deployment helper artifacts.
